attempt: Close the logical micro-lemma behind Ticket 25: a finite prefix certificate cannot by itself prove an all-height RH-equivalent statement unless it is paired with a theorem covering the unchecked tail.
formal_micro_lemma_statement: If a proposed RH route verifies only a finite prefix P and supplies no theorem for n>P or heights outside the checked window, then the route is not a universal proof certificate.

micro_lemma_certificate.proof_reason: Universal quantification cannot be discharged by a finite prefix without a separate tail theorem.

remaining_obligation: A genuine RH proof still needs an all-height zeta-zero or equivalent positivity theorem.
claim_boundary: No RH proof and no certified RH counterexample.
